name: stronghold-lookout
purpose: Operator tooling repo for Stronghold, now spanning both the Go CLI runtime and a React/TypeScript web cockpit for same-origin auth, NATS transport, and estate visibility.
status: active CLI plus web cockpit repo with principal-aware session state, production-shaped browser shell, read-first authority/edge surfaces, controlled authority mutations, and key lifecycle audit readback
repo_role_in_system: Operator control surface and config tooling for the Stronghold estate
depends_on: Stronghold principal/key authority and envelope trust posture

service_boundaries.cli_runtime: Go/Cobra CLI for login, whoami, config get/set/push, message capture, and operator workflows over NATS.
service_boundaries.web_shell: React/Vite browser cockpit with shell layout, static module registry, and read-first Overview, Sentry, Aegis, and Authority operator surfaces.
service_boundaries.session_client: Same-origin browser session boundary that opens Drawbridge auth, listens for callback completion, refreshes /_/auth/session, calls same-origin logout, and normalizes separate root and active principal metadata.
service_boundaries.authority_read_client: Typed same-origin HTTP client for Sentry authority read surfaces under /_/authority/read without browser-visible transport credentials, including badge archive state, grant provenance, key posture, and authority audit-event readback.
service_boundaries.authority_mutation_client: Typed same-origin HTTP client for controlled Sentry authority mutations under /_/authority/mutate with idempotency keys and operator confirmation states, including badge-definition, principal-badge grant, and principal-key lifecycle controls.
service_boundaries.nats_browser_transport: NATS over WebSocket browser rail used by the cockpit through /_/nats only when the Aegis-mediated browser NATS authorization model is implemented and marked ready; the current shell reports transport posture honestly without passing raw secrets.
service_boundaries.config_templates: Tracked JSON files remain examples/templates rather than live estate truth.

critical_invariants: Tracked JSON files are templates/examples, not live secret-bearing runtime configs.
Live edge config should come from generated Stronghold runtime artifacts, then be pushed via Lookout.
The web cockpit assumes same-origin auth under /_/auth and NATS transport under /_/nats.
The browser shell must not depend on reading the HttpOnly auth cookie directly; it consumes bootstrap/session payloads instead.
The browser shell must not receive or forward reusable transport credentials from session bootstrap payloads.
Static module registration is intentional and keeps the cockpit structure stable without runtime plugin machinery.

Lookout displays principal, badge, key, audit, and transport posture and can submit controlled Sentry mutations; it does not own principal key registration, envelope verification, badge truth, audit truth, or authority issuance.
Read-first Sentry/Aegis pages should stay honest about backend gaps rather than implying mature browser-admin APIs exist.
known_risks: Authority cockpit reads depend on Sentry same-origin HTTP adapters and Aegis-projected identity headers; if the live edge config does not project those headers, authorized sessions can still be denied.
The browser transport depends on the /_/nats rail being exposed through the Aegis-mediated browser authorization model; raw NATS credentials must remain server-side.
Audit reads currently surface key lifecycle mutation evidence; broader mutation/session auditing and transport commands remain intentionally deferred.
Template drift from generated runtime config is possible if bootstrap behavior changes and tracked examples are not refreshed.
Repo documentation still needs cleanup where older prose says no session bootstrap exists or says clients are unimplemented; the map reflects the current code, not those stale lines.
As Stronghold envelope enforcement becomes live, Lookout will need explicit UI/runtime posture for signed message authorship rather than only transport readiness.
